meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template for No-Design SaaS
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no design skills' hurdle. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting CSS and layout constraints. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain a production-ready, modular architecture that handles UI aesthetics automatically. Combined with Claude Code’s iterative generation, you can prototype, refine, and deploy high-converting SaaS interfaces in hours, not weeks—perfect for freelancers juggling multiple client projects.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the client deadline is looming, and your design skills are virtually non-existent. The pressure to ship a professional-grade SaaS interface is suffocating, and every hour spent tweaking margins is an hour of lost revenue. You don't need a design degree; you need a system that forces excellence by default.

Antigravity is that system. It acts as a structural exoskeleton for your code, providing a pre-built, high-conversion design framework that eliminates the guesswork. When you pair this with the raw power of Claude Code, you transition from a struggling developer into a high-velocity machine, delivering premium results that satisfy even the most demanding stakeholders.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the base architecture. This ensures your project starts with standardized component libraries and responsive layouts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity SaaS template with Tailwind CSS, authentication, and a responsive dashboard layout.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map out the specific features required by your client, ensuring the boilerplate is customized for their domain.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the core database schemas for a project management SaaS including users, tasks, and project categories.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Add Prisma schema for User, Project, and Task models with relations."

steps.title: Automate UI Component Injection
steps.description: Fill the template with functional components. Let the AI handle the visual polish while you focus on data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-conversion landing page and a user dashboard using the existing Antigravity design tokens.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write "Create a responsive dashboard sidebar and a main content area with a data table component."

steps.title: Integrate API Endpoints
steps.description: Connect your frontend to backend services seamlessly, ensuring the template handles loading states and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create API routes for CRUD operations on project tasks, ensuring all responses follow the standard JSON structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Generate Next.js API routes for task management with validation."

steps.title: Final Polish & Deployment
steps.description: Review the build, run accessibility checks, and push to production with confidence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Conduct an accessibility and mobile responsiveness audit of the current build and suggest CSS fixes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Audit the current UI for accessibility issues and fix mobile menu spacing."

pro_tips: Treat Antigravity as your 'design system'—don't write custom CSS unless you absolutely have to; rely on utility classes.
Use Claude Code's 'history' feature to revert to known-good states if the UI layout breaks during complex refactoring.
Always define your design tokens in the Antigravity config file first so the AI stays consistent across all generated pages.
Leverage Claude Code to write unit tests for your components to prevent regressions during rapid iterations.
Keep your prompt granularity high: ask for specific component structures rather than 'make it look better'.
content.next_steps: Once your base is deployed, focus on adding specialized client-side integrations or custom API hooks to differentiate your SaaS from generic templates.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to bypass design hurdles and ship professional software at scale. Stop overthinking the visuals and start shipping the value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
